title: Smoky Ethiopian Padron Pepper Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Ethiopian stew featuring smoky padron peppers, slow-cooked to perfection in a rich and aromatic berbere spice blend. This low-sodium dish is perfect for a chilly evening.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the padron peppers and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the berbere spice, smoked paprika, and salt-free seasoning blend. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the red wine (if using) and vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to simmer, stirring occasionally, until the stew has thickened and the flavors have melded together, about 20 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs (if desired).
